sentence: Subjective:
The patient, Jeremy, is a 30-year-old male who injured his lower back approximately 4 months ago while moving boxes during his son's college move-out.
He reports a history of chronic lower back pain, which worsens with movement, particularly when lifting heavy objects.
He describes a sharp pain in his lower back when rolling over in his sleep, which subsides with positional changes.
The patient denies numbness or tingling in his feet since the initial injury.
He has tried over-the-counter pain medications, including Tylenol and ibuprofen, with limited relief.
He reports some improvement with ibuprofen, but it is short-lived.
The patient has not experienced any stomach upset from ibuprofen.
He denies any symptoms of urinary or bowel incontinence.
He has a past medical history of a total knee replacement in his right knee 2 years ago, which was a successful surgery with minimal complications.
Past medical history:
The patient has a history of a total knee replacement in his right knee in the spring of 2020, which was followed by physical therapy and has been functioning at 95% capacity since then.
Review of systems:
The patient denies any fever, chills, or other systemic symptoms.
He reports some difficulty carrying heavy objects due to lower back pain, but otherwise reports no other symptoms.
Current medications:
I prescribed Meloxicam 15mg once daily for lower back pain.
Objective:
Vital signs:
Blood pressure: 117/56
Pulse rate: 68
Oxygen saturation: 95% on room air
Temperature: normal
Physical exam:
The patient has bony protuberance of C5-C6 without pain on palpation.
There is no pain to palpation of the lumbar spine.
There is decreased flexion and extension of the lower back.
The patient has a positive straight leg raise.
There is some spasm of the paraspinal muscles of the lumbar spine.
The lower extremities have equal strength bilaterally.
Assessment / Problem List:
Assessment: The patient has an acute disk herniation in the lumbar spine, which is likely the cause of his chronic lower back pain and related symptoms.
Problem list: Acute disk herniation; lower back pain; chronic pain; lumbar spine condition; suspected disk herniation.
Plan:
I ordered a lumbar spine MRI to further evaluate the extent of the disk herniation.
I will continue with physical therapy through our health system.
I prescribed Meloxicam 15mg once daily to help manage the patient's pain.
I will discuss further treatment options, including an epidural steroid injection, if the MRI confirms a herniated disk.
Follow-up:
The patient will follow up with me after the results of the lumbar spine MRI are available.
